name: VortexType
description: VortexType is a free touch-typing tutor from Techbridge University College. It teaches keyboarding through progressive lessons, timed speed tests that track words-per-minute and accuracy, and typing games, guided by a colour-coded on-screen keyboard that shows which finger to use. It installs as an offline-capable web app for low-connectivity classrooms.
features: Progressive touch-typing lessons that unlock as accuracy improves, from the home row to the full keyboard and numeric keypad
Timed speed tests with live words-per-minute and accuracy tracking
Typing games, including a Shark Attack arcade round, for practice that holds attention
A colour-coded on-screen keyboard with hand guidance showing the correct finger for every key
Installable, offline-capable web app for low-connectivity classrooms
audience: Students and anyone learning or improving touch typing, from beginners to speed-focused typists

faqs.a: VortexType is a free touch-typing tutor from Techbridge University College. It teaches keyboarding through progressive lessons, speed tests, and games, with an on-screen keyboard that shows which finger to use for each key.
Anyone learning or improving touch typing, from complete beginners working through the home row to typists building speed. It suits classroom use and self-paced practice alike.

faqs.q: Does it measure typing speed and accuracy?
faqs.a: Yes. Timed speed tests track words-per-minute and accuracy live, and lessons unlock progressively as accuracy improves.
Yes, it is free to use. It also installs as an app and works offline, so it runs in low-connectivity classrooms.
It was built by the ICT department of Techbridge University College in Accra, Ghana.
